page: Amazon Web Services r6id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The system provides 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, alongside 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. This configuration yields a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled as 3800 GB of NVMe SSD, and network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and specialized execution capabilities. The instance achieves a strong multi-core CPU score of 61947.22 in stress-ng and a PassMark CPU score of 57778.08. Cryptographic and database operations perform in the top tier, with PassMark database operations reaching 26558.56 and encryption scoring 56545.3. Software compilation with Clang and ray tracing benchmarks also register in the top 25% tier. Conversely, single-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as the 166.18 GB/sec read-only large block memory bandwidth, fall within the average range.

From a resource density perspective, the r6id.16xlarge offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads that require high-speed local NVMe storage. The inclusion of 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD reduces the need for external block storage attachments, improving cost efficiency for data-heavy operations. However, workloads requiring high single-core CPU throughput or massive memory bandwidth may find the performance average relative to the hardware class, representing a tradeoff for applications that do not scale horizontally across its 64 vCPUs.

This instance is configured for memory-intensive and storage-reliant workloads. Appropriate use cases include relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caching systems, data compilation pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 platform includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU execution, database operations, and cryptographic performance, alongside average single-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core combined with substantial local NVMe storage makes this instance cost-efficient for data-intensive workloads by reducing reliance on external storage. It is suited for database hosting, software compilation, in-memory caching, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database benchmark performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage, and balanced multi-core performance.

3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 3.5 GHz clock speed
